items.title: Hantavirus : les rongeurs capturés dans la province de Mendoza n'appartiennent pas à l'espèce potentiellement porteuse de la souche
items.title_en: Hantavirus : les rongeurs capturés dans la province de Mendoza n'appartiennent pas à l'espèce potentiellement porteuse de la souche
items.body: La province de Mendoza n'a pas de circulation avérée de la souche "Andes" de l'hantavirus impliquée sur le "MV Hondius".
items.body_en: La province de Mendoza n'a pas de circulation avérée de la souche "Andes" de l'hantavirus impliquée sur le "MV Hondius".

items.title: [World Report] The medicine of…cruise ships
items.title_en: [World Report] The medicine of…cruise ships
items.body: Although the hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius has drawn the world's attention, day-to-day practice is usually a little more prosaic. Talha Burki embarks on an examination of cruise ships.

items.title_en: Communicable disease threats report, 6 - 12 June 2026, Week 24
items.body: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 6-12 June, and includes updates on measles, Nipah virus infection, Chikungunya virus disease, Hantavirus, Ebola, and West Nile virus.

items.title: Data is not enough: from Covid to measles, America must relearn risk communication | Lynne Peeples
items.title_en: Data is not enough: from Covid to measles, America must relearn risk communication | Lynne Peeples
items.body: The confusion over hantavirus and Ebola is a reminder that we must do better at explaining how to respond to an outbreak Two unfolding outbreaks continue to command global attention. As a hantavirus outbreak tied to a cruise ship appears to be petering out, Ebola cases continue to mount in Africa. Alongside them have…
